title: acer tutcheri subsp. formosanum
intro: Acer tutcheri subsp. formosanum, also known as Formosan paperbark maple or Formosan paperbark, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is primarily valued for its unique, exfoliating bark which peels off in strips, revealing a smooth, grayish-brown surface underneath. Acer tutcheri subsp. formosanum has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is used to prepare herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are believed to have health benefits including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting immune function.
